perturbation.original_text: 5.3.1 Obligations of the Company 5.3.1.1 As a counterpart to the terms and conditions (including pricing conditions) agreed between the Parties under this Agreement [*****]. In the event, the Company fails to do so, the Repairer shall be entitled to revise such terms and conditions in order to take into account such failure.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3.1 Obligations of the Company 5.3.1.1 As a counterpart to the terms and conditions (including pricing conditions) agreed between the Parties under this Agreement [*****]. In the event, the Company fails to do so, the Repairer can not revise such terms and conditions in order to take into account such failure.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall' to 'can not' it creates an in-text contradiction with the general scope of the contract that gives rights to the repairer. The initial statement provided the Repairer with the right to revise the terms and conditions. The modified version restricts the Repairer from revising the terms, directly contradicting the original intent of the clause. This would cause uncertainty regarding the Repairer's rights, as it is not clear to what extent it can enforce the terms of the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3.1 Obligations of the Company 5.3.1.1 As a counterpart to the terms and conditions (including pricing conditions) agreed between the Parties under this Agreement [*****]. In the event, the Company fails to do so, the Repairer can not revise such terms and conditions in order to take into account such failure.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'shall' to 'can not,' directly reversing the Repairer's right to revise terms if the Company fails to meet its obligations. This creates a clear and direct conflict with the original intention of the clause, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, meaning the contradiction is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. WARRANTIES Upon Delivery, and subject to the terms of this Agreement, including the disclaimers and limitations on liability set forth in such warranties and in Clause 14 ("Disclaimer") of this Agreement, the Company shall be entitled to the benefit of the following warranties: i) For new LRUs or Main Elements, the terms and conditions set into the related Vendor Warranty Manual and in ATR72-600 Aircraft inthe relevant clauses of the relevant Aircraft sale and purchase agreement, shall apply. ii) For used LRUs and Main Elements repaired and overhauled by the Repairer, the warranty period shall start on the date of Delivery and shall end [*****] thereafter, whichever occurs the earliest, and such warranty shall be subject to the exclusions of warranty set forth in Exhibit 10 ("LRUs Repair Service") and in Exhibit 11 ("Main Elements Services").
perturbation.changed_text: 9. WARRANTIES Upon Delivery, and subject to the terms of this Agreement, including the disclaimers and limitations on liability set forth in such warranties and in Clause 14 ("Disclaimer") of this Agreement, the Company shall not be entitled to any warranties: i) For new LRUs or Main Elements, the terms and conditions set into the related Vendor Warranty Manual and in ATR72-600 Aircraft inthe relevant clauses of the relevant Aircraft sale and purchase agreement, shall apply. ii) For used LRUs and Main Elements repaired and overhauled by the Repairer, the warranty period shall start on the date of Delivery and shall end [*****] thereafter, whichever occurs the earliest, and such warranty shall be subject to the exclusions of warranty set forth in Exhibit 10 ("LRUs Repair Service") and in Exhibit 11 ("Main Elements Services").
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be entitled to the benefit of the following warranties' to 'shall not be entitled to any warranties' it introduces an in-text contradiction, creating ambiguity about whether the Company receives warranty benefits and directly contrasts with the subsequent clauses outlining warranty terms. The original text provided warranties to the Company, but the modified version takes that away creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. WARRANTIES Upon Delivery, and subject to the terms of this Agreement, including the disclaimers and limitations on liability set forth in such warranties and in Clause 14 ("Disclaimer") of this Agreement, the Company shall not be entitled to any warranties: i) For new LRUs or Main Elements, the terms and conditions set into the related Vendor Warranty Manual and in ATR72-600 Aircraft inthe relevant clauses of the relevant Aircraft sale and purchase agreement, shall apply. ii) For used LRUs and Main Elements repaired and overhauled by the Repairer, the warranty period shall start on the date of Delivery and shall end [*****] thereafter, whichever occurs the earliest, and such warranty shall be subject to the exclusions of warranty set forth in Exhibit 10 ("LRUs Repair Service") and in Exhibit 11 ("Main Elements Services").
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Clause 9 explicitly states that 'the Company shall not be entitled to any warranties,' while subsequent sub-clauses (i and ii) still refer to warranty terms, conditions, and exclusions. This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the clause itself. The contradiction is strong because it introduces uncertainty regarding the company's warranty rights. Even though the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same in this example, the explanation makes it clear that by changing the original text of 'shall be entitled to the benefit of the following warranties' to 'shall not be entitled to any warranties' caused a contradiction within the clause, as shown by the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
